purpose: Test a null-first hippocampal metacontroller that reuses a healthy null-context prefix HPC and invokes dynamic context only when causal retrieval contradiction is high.

score.name: null_prefix_retrieval_contradiction_cvar
score.interpretation: positive means the null-context HPC retrieval is worse than making no retrieval claim
score.aggregation: mean of the largest ceil(0.10 * prefix_length) per-step contradictions
score.retrieval_timing: read before writing current sensory value
score.decision_direction: score >= threshold invokes inferred dynamic context; otherwise retain null context
score.threshold_rule: 0.5 * (median calibration T1 score + median calibration T2 score)
score.choice_rationale: sparse wrong retrievals must not be diluted by all-token averaging; CVaR is frozen before observing any null-first score

state_machine.null_decision: retain the exact prefix HPC state and continue without replay
state_machine.dynamic_decision: discard the null prefix state, replay the observed prefix once with inferred dynamic context, then retain only that selected HPC state

allowed: action history through frozen EC/place/context pathways
past and current sensory observations inside the 72-step observed prefix
read-before-write retrieval and value energy from the single transient null-prefix HPC

post_result_rule.all_gates_pass: replace G1 as the preferred context-need controller and freeze a multi-checkpoint confirmation
post_result_rule.any_signal_gate_fails: stop null-first score family; do not tune prefix, CVaR fraction, threshold, formula, or seeds on G2 data
post_result_rule.signal_passes_but_efficiency_fails: retain G1 scientific result; localize state-reuse implementation without changing the score
